messages.text:
[CONCEPT SUMMARY]: GDPR compliance requires organizations (controllers) to implement robust technical and organizational measures to protect personal data, including data protection policies, pseudonymisation, and encryption. They must also be able to demonstrate that valid consent was obtained from data subjects and provide easy mechanisms for consent withdrawal.

[LEGAL CITATION]: Source: Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR), Article 5(3) and Article 70(1)(e); Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act), Recital 69.

[PRACTICAL IMPLICATION]:
*   Companies must integrate data protection principles like data minimization and privacy by design into their systems and processes.
*   Organizations need to maintain clear records of consent obtained from individuals and ensure that consent can be easily withdrawn.
*   Technical measures such as encryption and pseudonymisation should be employed to safeguard personal data throughout its lifecycle.
